title: The Tidal Frequency Palace

features.name: seagull grid
features.desc-on-touch: a creature rendered in exactly 11 polygons, floating above the grid as a sparse approximation of flight — the fewer the faces, the more it seems to remember the air that shaped it

features.name: frequency matrix
features.desc-on-touch: the grid speaks in sound — each line is a tone, each intersection is a harmonic — the palace hums with the tides' own scale, where three heartbeats equal one wave

features.name: tidal argument
features.desc-on-touch: two columns of numbers that contradict each other perfectly — one shows the tide coming in, the other shows it going out, both records dated today — the archive cannot decide which is the lie

features.name: heartbeat theorem
features.desc-on-touch: a plaque stating the law: Surgery = three heartbeats; Beach = one heartbeat; therefore the archive's pulse runs at a frequency the ocean and the scalpel can both read

features.name: poetry converted to waveform
features.desc-on-touch: lines of verse encoded as spectral data — when played through a speaker, they sound like wind across water; when read as text, they sound like the wind's own argument with itself

inscription: here the storm's thought becomes readable as it reverses itself, each descending frequency a word in the archive's slow certainty

file.name: Tidal_Frequency_Spectral_Archive_QR_Encoded.bin
file.note: tidal tables merged with heartbeat data merged with spectral analysis of a nine-hour polyphonic recording — the file refuses to render as pure data; when decoded it becomes both music and geography

exits.label: toward a shore where all measurements become one
up to the diagnostic lighthouse that counts every wave
down to the bedrock where stone learns to sing back
